authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Lily Pond not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Lily Pond not listed; the Lake Tahoe tributary entry (152) reaches flowing tributaries only)
sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Lake layer record for gaz_id 1733775, "Lily Pond", Lake, El Dorado County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":1733775,"gaz_name":"Lily Pond","gaz_featureclass":"Lake","fcode":39001,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"El Dorado"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13376424.8692, 4726647.8325) converts to lon -120.162469, lat 39.034732, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-120.16243, 39.03474) to within ~10m.

parameters.note: Lily Pond is a small pond in the General Creek drainage (General Creek is a Lake Tahoe tributary), reached by trail from Ed Z'berg Sugar Pine Point State Park. It is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so the general statewide lake regulation §5.85(a)(1) governs — open all year, 5 trout/10. The Lake Tahoe tributary catch-and-release closure in §7.50(b)(152) applies only to the flowing Tahoe tributaries 'upstream to the first lake,' not to a standalone pond, so it does not reach Lily Pond. No fish species or stocking are asserted: the pond is absent from CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document and no current authoritative source names fish in it.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.
